impact: The CSP middleware currently emits Report-Only headers but has no report-uri / report-to directive — violations only surface in browser DevTools, never server-side. Adding a /csp-report endpoint that logs violations to a jsonl file would let us automate before/after diffs during T-031/T-032 migrations and catch regressions earlier. ~30 lines: FastAPI route accepting application/csp-report POSTs, appending to a rotating log under /data/logs/. Reference vigilant/app/middleware/csp_nonce.py for the existing header template.
resolution:
Added a server-side CSP violation sink.

- **app/routes/csp.py** (new) — `POST /csp-report` endpoint. Accepts both legacy `application/csp-report` payloads (`{"csp-report": {...}}`) and modern Reporting-API format (array of `{type, body}`). Appends one JSON line per violation to `/data/logs/csp-violations.jsonl` with truncated field values (cap doc/blocked/source at 512 chars, UA at 256, directive at 64). One-level size-capped rotation at 10 MB → `csp-violations.jsonl.1` so a regression burst can't fill the disk. Always returns 204; all errors swallowed.
- **app/middleware/csp_nonce.py** — added `report-uri /csp-report` to `_CSP_TEMPLATE`. Browsers will now POST violation reports back to the app.
- **app/main.py** — wired csp_router into the app.

Effect: T-031 / T-033 migration work can now diff `wc -l /data/logs/csp-violations.jsonl` before/after a template change to spot regressions without manually walking every page in DevTools. The 24–48h Report-Only soak step in T-033's runway now has automated telemetry behind it.

Commit: pending deploy.
title: CSP report endpoint for violation telemetry
